📖 English

Use of Pronouns

Replace nouns with these handy little words - pronouns!

📚 Chapter 2 ⏱️ 2 min read

Unit 2: Use of Pronouns

A pronoun is a word that takes the place of a noun. Instead of saying “Ram is tall because Ram exercises,” we say “Ram is tall because he exercises.”

Types of Pronouns

1. Personal Pronouns

These refer to specific people or things.

PersonSingularPlural
First (speaking)I, mewe, us
Second (listening)youyou
Third (talking about)he, him, she, her, itthey, them

Example: I love ice cream. They are coming tomorrow.

2. Demonstrative Pronouns

These point to specific things.

Example: This is my pen. Those are your books.

SingularPlural
thisthese
thatthose

3. Indefinite Pronouns

These refer to non-specific people or things.

Example: Somebody is at the door. Everyone enjoyed the party.

Common indefinite pronouns: some, any, all, nobody, somebody, everyone, nothing

4. Interrogative Pronouns

These are used to ask questions.

Example: What is your name? Which color do you like? Who is there?

5. Relative Pronouns

These connect a clause to a noun.

Example: The girl who won the race is my friend. This is the book that I bought.

6. Reflexive Pronouns

These refer back to the subject of the sentence. They end in -self or -selves.

Example: I hurt myself. She made herself a sandwich.

SingularPlural
myselfourselves
yourselfyourselves
himself, herself, itselfthemselves

💡 Tip: Use reflexive pronouns only when the subject and object are the same person!

Try It Yourself!

  1. Replace the noun: “Sita is a doctor.” → ”______ is a doctor.”
  2. Choose the correct pronoun: “Myself/I made this cake.”
  3. Fill in the blank: ”______ is knocking? (Who/Which)”

Pronouns make our sentences shorter and smoother. Practice using them every day!

🎯
